finding.summary: Track true peak is at -0.1 dBTP, essentially at the digital ceiling, with no FX on this bus to control inter-sample peaks. The track is named 'Parallel Drum Bus' but the FX chain is empty, so the measured hot level and 9 dB crest factor were baked in upstream of this track — the chain itself cannot be the source of that character. Capture is reliable (silence_fraction 0.05), so the levels reflect real signal.
finding.probable_cause: Audio rendered upstream with limiting/parallel compression already applied; this bus is just passing that hot signal through with 0.0 dB fader and no margin left for summing, dithering, or lossy codec conversion downstream.
finding.confidence_reason: true_peak_db and sample_peak_db are directly measured; silence_fraction 0.05 means the level statistics are not diluted. The FX chain is verifiably empty in the payload.

evidence_refs.description: True peak -0.1 dBTP leaves essentially no headroom.
Sample peak -0.3 dB confirms signal is near full scale.
9 dB crest factor consistent with already-limited material.
Empty FX chain; no limiter on this bus to catch inter-sample peaks.
Naming implies parallel compression character that is not produced by any current plugin.

proposal.reason: Pulling the fader down 1 dB creates true-peak headroom on the bus without changing the signal character or removing any processing. This is the only move directly supported by the measured -0.1 dBTP and the empty FX chain.
